159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())
1823	    arguments["model"]        = self
1824	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
1827	    del arguments["self"]
1828	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
1829	    del arguments["first_conversion"]
1830	
1831	    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
1832	    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)
1833	
1834	    # Non blocking install GGUF first
1835	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
1836	
1837	        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
1838	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
1839	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1840	            python_install.wait()
1841	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
1842	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1843	            makefile = None
1844	        else:
1845	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
1846	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1847	            git_clone.wait()
1848	            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
1849	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1850	            python_install.wait()
1851	        pass
1852	    else:
1853	        try:
1854	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1855	            makefile = None
1856	        except:
1857	            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
1858	            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:

193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.

198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]

2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:

2603	            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
2605	                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
2606	        else:
2607	            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
2608	        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:

70	@torch.inference_mode()
71	def generate_responses(
72	    model,
73	    tokenizer,
74	    prompt,
75	    max_new_tokens: int = 100,
76	    temperature: float = 0.8,
77	    do_sample: bool = True,
78	    num_generations: int = 1,
79	    skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
80	    dtype: torch.dtype = None,
81	):
82	    inputs = [tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors="pt") for _ in range(num_generations)]
83	    keys = inputs[0].keys()
84	    batched_inputs = {
85	        key: torch.cat([input[key] for input in inputs], dim=0).to(model.device)
86	        for key in keys
87	    }
88	
89	    if dtype is not None:
90	        inference_context = torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=dtype)
91	    else:
92	        inference_context = nullcontext()
93	
94	    with inference_context:
95	        outputs = model.generate(
96	            **batched_inputs,
97	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
98	            do_sample=do_sample,
99	            temperature=temperature,
100	        )
101	
102	    responses = tokenizer.batch_decode(outputs, skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens)
103	    return responses
104

229	def convert_weights_back_to_dtype(model, dtype):
230	    """
231	    SFTTrainer calls get_peft_model and prepare_model_for_kbit_training which converts all weights to float32.
232	    This function converts the non-loraweights back to the original dtype.

236	            param.data = param.data.to(dtype)

248	def replace_module(
249	    module: torch.nn.Module,
250	    target_module_type: torch.nn.Module,
251	    conversion_func: Callable,
252	):
253	    for child_name, child_module in module.named_children():
254	        if isinstance(child_module, target_module_type):
255	            new_module = conversion_func(child_module)
256	            setattr(module, child_name, new_module)
257	        else:
258	            replace_module(child_module, target_module_type, conversion_func)
259	
260	
261	def _convert_lora_to_linear(module: LoraLayer, adapter_name: str = "default"):
262	    base_layer = module.get_base_layer()
263	    weight = base_layer.weight
264	
265	    assert isinstance(weight, bnb.nn.Params4bit)
266	    quant_state = weight.quant_state
267	    original_dtype = quant_state.dtype
268	
269	    w_dq = dequantize_4bit(weight.data, quant_state).float()
270	    lora_delta = (
271	        module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
272	        @ module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
273	        * module.scaling[adapter_name]
274	    )
275	    w_dq += lora_delta.float()
276	    w_dq = w_dq.to(original_dtype)
277	
278	    new_module = torch.nn.Linear(
279	        w_dq.shape[1], w_dq.shape[0], bias=module.base_layer.bias is not None
280	    )
281	    new_module.weight.data = torch.nn.Parameter(w_dq, requires_grad=False)
282	    if module.lora_bias[adapter_name]:
283	        bias_data = module.base_layer.bias.data + module.lora_B[adapter_name].bias
284	        new_module.bias.data = torch.nn.Parameter(bias_data, requires_grad=False)
285	    return new_module
286	
287	
288	def convert_lora_to_linear(model: torch.nn.Module):
289	    replace_module(model, LoraLayer, _convert_lora_to_linear)
290	    assert not any(isinstance(module, LoraLayer) for module in model.modules())
291	    return model

849	        text = "if weight is not None:\n"

851	        end = source.find("self.to(weight.device)", start)
852	        spaces = re.findall(r"^([ ]{1,})break", source, flags = re.MULTILINE)[0]
853	        source = source.replace(source[start : end], spaces)
854	        spaces = len(re.match(r"[\s]{1,}", source).group(0))
855	        lines = source.split("\n")
856	        source = "\n".join(x[spaces:] for x in lines)
857	        source = re.sub(r"([^\.])nn\.", r"\1torch.nn.", source)

888	        elif "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames: statistics = "kaggle"
889	        elif "\nRUNPOD_" in keynames: statistics = "runpod"
890	        elif "\nAWS_"    in keynames: statistics = "aws"
891	        elif "\nAZURE_"  in keynames: statistics = "azure"

911	                        elif "google"                in file_content: return "gcp"
912	                return "other"
913	            pass
914	            try:    statistics = try_vllm_check()
915	            except: statistics = "other"
916	        pass

926	        pass

25	def search_models(org: str = None, base_name: str = None, version: str = None, size: str = None, quant_types: list[QuantType] = None, search_pattern: str = None) -> list[ModelInfo]:
26	    """
27	    Get model info from the registry.
28	
29	    See registry.ModelInfo for more fields.
30	
31	    If search_pattern is provided, the full model path will be matched against the pattern, where the model path is the model_id on huggingface hub.
32	
33	    """
34	    if not _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
35	        register_models()
36	    
37	    model_infos = MODEL_REGISTRY.values()
38	    if org:
39	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.org == org]
40	    if base_name:
41	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.base_name == base_name]
42	    if version:
43	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.version == version]
44	    if size:
45	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.size == size]
46	    if quant_types:
47	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if any(model_info.quant_type == quant_type for quant_type in quant_types)]
48	    if search_pattern:
49	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if search_pattern in model_info.model_path]
50	    
51	    return model_infos

1981	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",

261	def _convert_lora_to_linear(module: LoraLayer, adapter_name: str = "default"):
262	    base_layer = module.get_base_layer()
263	    weight = base_layer.weight
264	
265	    assert isinstance(weight, bnb.nn.Params4bit)
266	    quant_state = weight.quant_state
267	    original_dtype = quant_state.dtype
268	
269	    w_dq = dequantize_4bit(weight.data, quant_state).float()
270	    lora_delta = (
271	        module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
272	        @ module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
273	        * module.scaling[adapter_name]
274	    )
275	    w_dq += lora_delta.float()
276	    w_dq = w_dq.to(original_dtype)
277	
278	    new_module = torch.nn.Linear(
279	        w_dq.shape[1], w_dq.shape[0], bias=module.base_layer.bias is not None
280	    )
281	    new_module.weight.data = torch.nn.Parameter(w_dq, requires_grad=False)
282	    if module.lora_bias[adapter_name]:
283	        bias_data = module.base_layer.bias.data + module.lora_B[adapter_name].bias
284	        new_module.bias.data = torch.nn.Parameter(bias_data, requires_grad=False)
285	    return new_module
286	
287	
288	def convert_lora_to_linear(model: torch.nn.Module):
289	    replace_module(model, LoraLayer, _convert_lora_to_linear)
290	    assert not any(isinstance(module, LoraLayer) for module in model.modules())
291	    return model

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

490

493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight

501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result

Perfect! Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive report. Let me compile all the findings:

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Unsloth Project

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE OVERVIEW

**Top-level Directory:**
```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point, imports and patches
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── chat_templates.py           # 129KB - Chat template handling
├── import_fixes.py             # Import-time patches for dependencies
├── save.py                     # 105KB - Core saving/export functionality (CRITICAL)
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # 41KB - Tokenizer utilities + GGUF support
├── trainer.py                  # Training utilities
├── models/                     # Core model implementations
├── kernels/                    # Optimized CUDA/GPU kernels
├── registry/                   # Model registry system
├── dataprep/                   # Data preparation utilities
└── utils/                      # General utilities
```

**Key Subdirectories:**
- `/models/` - Contains 22 model architecture files
- `/kernels/` - Contains fast inference/training kernels including MOE support
- `/registry/` - Model registry system for discovering/registering models
- `/dataprep/` - Data preparation and preprocessing

---

### 2. MAIN MODEL CLASSES & ENTRY POINTS

**Class Hierarchy:**
```
FastLlamaModel (base class in llama.py)
├── FastLanguageModel (extends FastLlamaModel) - Main text model loader
├── FastModel (new unified loader in loader.py)
│   ├── FastVisionModel - Vision-language models
│   └── FastTextModel - Text-only models
├── FastMistralModel
├── FastQwen2Model / FastQwen3Model
├── FastGemmaModel / FastGemma2Model
├── FastGraniteModel
├── FastCohereModel
├── FastFalconH1Model
└── (others)
```

**Key Model Classes:**

1. **FastLanguageModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py:107`)
   - Primary entry point for text model loading
   - Static method: `from_pretrained()` with extensive parameters
   - Supports 4-bit QLoRA, 8-bit LoRA, 16-bit LoRA, and full fine-tuning

2. **FastModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py:526`)
   - Unified loader supporting both text and vision models
   - Calls `from_pretrained()` with model type detection

3. **FastVisionModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py:995`)

### 5. HOW MODEL CREATION & SAVING WORKS

**Typical Workflow:**

```python
# Step 1: Load model (auto-patches for optimization)
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
    model_name="unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
    max_seq_length=2048,
    dtype=torch.float16,
    load_in_4bit=True,  # BnB 4-bit quantization
)

# Step 2: Add LoRA adapters
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
    model, r=16, target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", ...]
)

# Step 3: Train (using optimized kernels)
trainer = SFTTrainer(...)
trainer.train()

# Step 4: Save (using patched methods)
model.save_pretrained_merged(              # Merge LoRA & save
    save_directory="./output",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)

# Alternative: Save as GGUF
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    save_directory="./gguf_output",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"  # llama.cpp format
)

# Alternative: Save with TorchAO quantization
model.save_pretrained_torchao(
    save_directory="./torchao_output",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    torchao_config=Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
)

# Alternative: Upload to Hub
model.push_to_hub_merged(
    repo_id="my_username/my_model",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)
```

---

### 6. INTERNAL SAVE FLOW

**When `save_pretrained_merged()` is called:**

1. `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 2413) is called
2. Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` which:
   - Merges LoRA weights using `_merge_lora()` function (line 159)
   - Dequantizes 4-bit weights if needed using `fast_dequantize()` from kernels
   - Saves in HuggingFace format (safetensors or pickle)
   - Handles sharding for large models (configurable max_shard_size)
   - Cleans up temporary buffers and manages memory

**When `save_pretrained_gguf()` is called:**

1. `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768) is called
2. First converts to merged 16-bit format (if needed)
3. Calls `save_to_gguf()` (line 952) which:
   - Installs llama.cpp if not present
   - Clones/builds llama.cpp repository with specific targets:
     - `llama-quantize` - Quantization tool
     - `llama-export-lora` - LoRA export
     - `llama-cli` - Inference CLI
   - Runs `llama-quantize` executable with chosen quantization method
   - Produces `.gguf` file suitable for llama.cpp inference

**When `save_pretrained_torchao()` is called:**

1. `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502) is called
2. First saves merged 16-bit copy to temporary location
3. Loads model using AutoModel
4. Applies TorchAO quantization config
5. Saves quantized version separately (with `-torchao` suffix)

---

### 7. REGISTRY SYSTEM

**Location:** `/unsloth/registry/`

**Components:**
- `registry.py` - Core registry with `MODEL_REGISTRY` dict and `ModelInfo` class
- `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_qwen.py`, `_gemma.py`, `_deepseek.py`, `_phi.py`
  - Model-specific registration functions

**API:** `search_models(org, base_name, version, size, quant_types, search_pattern)`

**Purpose:** Discover available pre-configured models for quick loading

---

### 8. ADAPTER/LoRA HANDLING

**LoRA Implementation:**
- Built on top of PEFT library
- Models can be loaded with LoRA adapters via `get_peft_model()`
- Supported merge methods:
  - Merging into 16-bit weights (for GGUF)
  - Merging into 4-bit weights (for continued training)
  - Saving as LoRA adapters only (no merge)

**Quantization Support:**
- BnB (Bits and Bytes): `Bnb_Linear4bit`
- PEFT: `Peft_Linear4bit`, `Peft_Linear`
- TorchAO: Via `torchao` library

---

### 9. ENVIRONMENT & DEPENDENCIES

**Critical Dependencies:**
- `torch` (2.0+) - GPU acceleration
- `transformers` (version-dependent features)
- `peft` - LoRA/adapter management
- `bitsandbytes` - 4-bit quantization
- `unsloth_zoo` - Registry and utility functions
- `huggingface_hub` - Model uploading

**Optional Dependencies:**
- `vllm` - Fast inference backend
- `torchao` - TorchAO quantization
- `llama.cpp` - GGUF conversion (auto-installed)

---

### 10. TEST COVERAGE

**Test Files:**
- `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` - Main save/merge tests
  - Tests: `save_merged_16bit`, `save_merged_4bit`, `save_torchao`
  - Validates model loading from saved formats
  - Inference tests with saved models

- `/tests/saving/language_models/` - Model-specific saving tests
  - Perplexity validation after merging
  - Push to hub with sharded indices
  - GRPO model saving

- `/tests/saving/vision_models/` - Vision model save tests

---

### 11. KEY INSIGHTS

1. **Patching Architecture**: Models receive dynamic method injection via `patch_saving_functions()`, making the API flexible and extensible

2. **Memory Efficiency**: The `_merge_lora()` function uses `addmm_()` for in-place addition to minimize memory overhead during merging

3. **Multi-format Export**: Supports HF format (safetensors/pickle), GGUF (llama.cpp), TorchAO, and LoRA-only

4. **Automatic Optimization**: Model loading automatically applies Unsloth's optimized kernels without user intervention

5. **Hub Integration**: Built-in Hugging Face Hub integration with automatic Unsloth attribution tagging

6. **Version Gating**: Feature availability tied to transformers version (e.g., `SUPPORTS_LLAMA31`, `SUPPORTS_GEMMA2`)

---

### 12. ABSOLUTE FILE PATHS (Key Files)

```
/unsloth/__init__.py                           # Entry point
/unsloth/save.py                               # Core export functionality
/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py                    # Tokenizer + GGUF utilities
/unsloth/chat_templates.py                     # Chat format handling
/unsloth/trainer.py                            # Training integration
/unsloth/models/__init__.py                    # Model class exports
/unsloth/models/loader.py                      # FastLanguageModel, FastModel
/unsloth/models/llama.py                       # FastLlamaModel (base)
/unsloth/models/_utils.py                      # Model utilities
/unsloth/models/mistral.py                     # Mistral model
/unsloth/models/gemma.py / gemma2.py           # Gemma models
/unsloth/models/qwen2.py / qwen3.py            # Qwen models
/unsloth/models/vision.py                      # Vision model base
/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py                  # LoRA kernels
/unsloth/kernels/utils.py                      # Fast dequant
/unsloth/registry/registry.py                  # Model registry
/unsloth/registry/_llama.py                    # Llama model registry
(and others for each model family)
```

## Comprehensive LoRA Adapter Weight Structure and Access Patterns in Unsloth

Based on thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's how LoRA adapter weights are structured, stored, and manipulated:

### 1. LoRA LAYER STRUCTURE

**LoRA Layers are PEFT Wrapper Classes** from the `peft` library. The main LoRA layer types in unsloth:

- **`Peft_Linear`** (FP32 LoRA layers)

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None:
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    return (
        W,                                    # Base weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),      # Quantization state
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,          # LoRA A matrix
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,          # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],                # Scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,                      # Original bias
    )
```

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)  # Dequantize if needed
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()  # Transpose for computation
    
    if A is not None:
        # Merge: W += s * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    return W.t().to(original_dtype), bias
```

### 6. LoRA WEIGHT EXTRACTION AND MERGING

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
```

### **3. LORA WEIGHT EXTRACTION IMPLEMENTATION**

#### **`get_lora_parameters` (Full precision)**
**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`
**Lines:** 190-232

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # Get base layer (handles DPO wrapped adapters)
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Optional fake quantization for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
    
    # Early exit if adapters disabled or merged
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract LoRA A and B matrices with optional QAT quantization
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],  # LoRA alpha/rank scale factor
    )
```

**Returns tuple of:**
1. `W`: Base layer weight tensor
2. `quant_state`: Quantization state (for 4bit) or None
3. `A`: LoRA A matrix (adapter rank × input dim)
4. `B`: LoRA B matrix (output dim × adapter rank)
5. `s`: LoRA scaling factor (alpha value)

### **4. LORA WEIGHT MERGING IMPLEMENTATION**

#### **`_merge_lora` (Core merge logic)**
**Lines:** 159-187

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        if quant_state is not None:
            # Dequantize 4bit weights to float32
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Key merge formula: W += (A.T @ B.T) * s
            # Using addmm_ for efficiency: W += alpha * A.T @ B.T
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
            
            # Validate merged weights are finite
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    
    return W, bias
```

**Merge Formula:**
- **Input:** W (d_out × d_in), A (d_in × r), B (d_out × r), s (scalar)
- **Computation:** W_merged = W + (A^T @ B^T)^T * s = W + B @ A^T * s (after transpose)
- **Output:** Merged weight matrix (d_out × d_in)

**Note:** Uses PyTorch's `addmm_` (add matrix-matrix product in-place) for memory efficiency

### **3. LORA WEIGHT EXTRACTION IMPLEMENTATION**

#### **`get_lora_parameters` (Full precision)**
**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`
**Lines:** 190-232

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # Get base layer (handles DPO wrapped adapters)
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Optional fake quantization for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
    
    # Early exit if adapters disabled or merged
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Extract LoRA A and B matrices with optional QAT quantization
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],  # LoRA alpha/rank scale factor
    )
```

**Returns tuple of:**
1. `W`: Base layer weight tensor
2. `quant_state`: Quantization state (for 4bit) or None
3. `A`: LoRA A matrix (adapter rank × input dim)
4. `B`: LoRA B matrix (output dim × adapter rank)
5. `s`: LoRA scaling factor (alpha value)

---

#### **`get_lora_parameters_bias` (With bias)**
**Lines:** 235-257

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # Identical to get_lora_parameters but also returns bias
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
```

**Returns 6-tuple** (same as above + bias at end)

---

### **4. LORA WEIGHT MERGING IMPLEMENTATION**

#### **`_merge_lora` (Core merge logic)**
**Lines:** 159-187

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        if quant_state is not None:
            # Dequantize 4bit weights to float32
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Key merge formula: W += (A.T @ B.T) * s
            # Using addmm_ for efficiency: W += alpha * A.T @ B.T
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
            
            # Validate merged weights are finite
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    
    return W, bias
```

**Merge Formula:**
- **Input:** W (d_out × d_in), A (d_in × r), B (d_out × r), s (scalar)
- **Computation:** W_merged = W + (A^T @ B^T)^T * s = W + B @ A^T * s (after transpose)
- **Output:** Merged weight matrix (d_out × d_in)

**Note:** Uses PyTorch's `addmm_` (add matrix-matrix product in-place) for memory efficiency

---

### **5. MERGED MODEL WEIGHT ITERATION**

#### **Weight Layer Constants**
**Lines:** 67-75

```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj",    # Query projection
    "self_attn.k_proj",    # Key projection  
    "self_attn.v_proj",    # Value projection
    "self_attn.o_proj",    # Output projection
    "mlp.gate_proj",       # Gate (SwiGLU)
    "mlp.up_proj",         # Up/intermediate projection
    "mlp.down_proj",       # Down projection
)

LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm",
    "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm",
    "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm",    # For Llama3.1
    "self_attn.k_norm",    # For Llama3.1
)
```

#### **Merged Model Save Loop**
**Lines:** 549-602

```python
# For each layer:
for j, layer in enumerate(ProgressBar(internal_model.model.layers)):
    
    # Process LoRA weights
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        name = f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"
        W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)  # <-- MERGE HAPPENS HERE
        
        # Save bias if exists
        if bias is not None:
            state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.bias"] = bias
        
        # Save weight (with memory optimization)
        if (torch.cuda.memory_allocated() + W.nbytes) < max_vram:
            state_dict[name] = W  # Save to GPU
        else:
            # Fallback to disk if running out of memory
            filename = os.path.join(temporary_location, f"{name}.pt")
            torch.save(W, filename, pickle_module=pickle, 
                      pickle_protocol=pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL)
            state_dict[name] = torch.load(filename, map_location="cpu", 
                                         mmap=True, weights_only=False)
    
    # Process layer norms (no merging needed)
    for item in LLAMA_LAYERNORMS:
        try:
            state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"] = eval(f"layer.{item}.weight.data")
        except:
            continue

# Also save embeddings and final norms
state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = internal_model.model.embed_tokens.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)
state_dict["model.norm.weight"] = internal_model.model.norm.weight.data
state_dict["lm_head.weight"] = internal_model.lm_head.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)  # If not tied
```

### **6. MERGING STRATEGIES FOR 4BIT MODELS**

#### **4bit Merge via PEFT**
**Lines:** 293-303

```python
    print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")

    print("Done.")
```

**Note:** `merge_and_unload()` is a PEFT method that merges LoRA adapters into base 4bit weights while maintaining quantization. The merged model is returned.

### **7. METHOD PATCHING TO MODEL**

#### **`patch_saving_functions`**
**Lines:** 2569-2668

Patches the following methods onto the model instance:

```python
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)

model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)

```

Also patches `push_to_hub` wrapper that adds "unsloth" tag.

---

### **8. ADAPTER STATE DICT HANDLING**

#### **LoRA-only save (Lines 444-448)**

```python
    save_pretrained_settings["selected_adapters"] = None  # Save only adapters
```

This tells PEFT to save only LoRA weights, not base model weights.

### **9. CONFIGURATION MODIFICATIONS FOR MERGED MODELS**

**Lines:** 682-693

```python
# Since merged, edit quantization_config
old_config = model.config
new_config = model.config.to_dict()
if "quantization_config" in new_config:
    del new_config["quantization_config"]  # Remove quantization config

while hasattr(original_model, "model"):
    original_model = original_model.model
    original_model.config = new_config
model.config = new_config
```

**Reason:** When merging 4bit weights into float16, quantization config becomes invalid.

gc.collect()

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass

    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]

input.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for adding ONNX export support for LoRA adapters in unsloth.

## Codebase Context

The unsloth project is located at: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395507305-8x6xcj/`

### Key File: `/unsloth/save.py` (~2679 lines)
This is the main save/export module. It contains:
- All export methods: GGUF, GGML, TorchAO, merged saves
- `__all__` list at line 48-54 exposing public functions
- `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2569-2678 that dynamically patches save methods onto model instances using `types.MethodType`
- Pattern: each export method is a standalone function taking `self` as first param, then bound to the model via `types.MethodType`

### How existing export methods work:

**GGML Export** (lines 2211-2262) - Closest analogy since it exports LoRA adapters in a format:
1. Function `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(self, save_directory, tokenizer, ...)`
2. First saves LoRA adapters using `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (which calls `unsloth_save_model` with `save_method="lora"`)
3. Then converts using an external tool
4. Registered at line 2667: `model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(fn, model)`

**TorchAO Export** (lines 2502-2561):
1. Function `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(self, save_directory, tokenizer, torchao_config, push_to_hub, token)`
2. First merges LoRA to 16bit via `unsloth_generic_save()`

### LoRA Weight Access Patterns:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    # Returns (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling) for a LoRA layer
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight  # shape: (rank, in_features)
    B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight  # shape: (out_features, rank)
    scaling = proj.scaling[adapter]  # float scalar
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)
```

Design an implementation plan for adding ONNX export support for LoRA adapters in unsloth.

## Codebase Context

The unsloth project is located at: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395507305-8x6xcj/`

### Key File: `/unsloth/save.py` (~2679 lines)
This is the main save/export module. It contains:
- All export methods: GGUF, GGML, TorchAO, merged saves
- `__all__` list at line 48-54 exposing public functions
- `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2569-2678 that dynamically patches save methods onto model instances using `types.MethodType`
- Pattern: each export method is a standalone function taking `self` as first param, then bound to the model via `types.MethodType`

### How existing export methods work:

**GGML Export** (lines 2211-2262) - Closest analogy since it exports LoRA adapters in a format:
1. Function `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(self, save_directory, tokenizer, ...)`
2. First saves LoRA adapters using `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (which calls `unsloth_save_model` with `save_method="lora"`)
3. Then converts using an external tool
4. Registered at line 2667: `model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(fn, model)`

**TorchAO Export** (lines 2502-2561):

3. Then applies TorchAO quantization

2609

2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag

2627	    pass
2628	
2629	    try:
2630	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2631	    except:
2632	        del arguments["tags"]
2633	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641	
2642	    original_model = model

2644

2646	            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub

2650	            pass

2652

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!

173	        if A is not None:

177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():

182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
